title: Sketch of Prahlad, The Great Devotee

analysis.core_story_summary: Pious virtues of Prahlad: - The youngest son of the demon king Hiranykashipu, Prahlad was very virtuous. He used to serve the sages with love. He treated all the beings equally and did never differentiate them as separate from him.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.
Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Sketch of Prahlad, The Great Devotee — Kids (8-12):** Pious virtues of Prahlad: - The youngest son of the demon king Hiranykashipu, Prahlad was very virtuous. He used to serve the sages with love. He treated all the beings equally and did never differentiate them as separate from him. This story teaches devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, the cost of conflict, inner focus and self-mastery. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **Sketch of Prahlad, The Great Devotee — Teens (13-17):** Pious virtues of Prahlad: - The youngest son of the demon king Hiranykashipu, Prahlad was very virtuous. He used to serve the sages with love. He treated all the beings equally and did never differentiate them as separate from him. For teens, the key idea is devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, the cost of conflict, inner focus and self-mastery. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.

He used to serve the sages with love. He treated all the beings equally and did never differentiate them as separate from him. A modern takeaway is devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, the cost of conflict, inner focus and self-mastery. redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **Sketch of Prahlad, The Great Devotee — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** Pious virtues of Prahlad: - The youngest son of the demon king Hiranykashipu, Prahlad was very virtuous. He used to serve the sages with love. He treated all the beings equally and did never differentiate them as separate from him. The episode highlights devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, the cost of conflict, inner focus and self-mastery. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **Sketch of Prahlad, The Great Devotee — Global General Audience:** Pious virtues of Prahlad: - The youngest son of the demon king Hiranykashipu, Prahlad was very virtuous. He used to serve the sages with love. He treated all the beings equally and did never differentiate them as separate from him. In universal terms, it explores devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, the cost of conflict, inner focus and self-mastery. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
